title: Aspirin Dosage for Acute Coronary Syndrome
content: For acute coronary syndrome (ACS), immediately administer a loading dose of 162 to 325 mg of aspirin. It should be chewed to ensure rapid absorption, especially in emergency settings without internet access where fast guidelines are critical.

title: Anaphylaxis Emergency Treatment
content: First-line treatment for an anaphylactic reaction is Epinephrine, 0.3 to 0.5 mg given intramuscularly (IM) into the anterolateral thigh. Repeat every 5 to 15 minutes if there is no clinical improvement.

title: Pediatric Paracetamol Guidelines
content: For children, paracetamol (acetaminophen) dosage is 10 to 15 mg/kg per dose, every 4 to 6 hours as needed for fever or pain. Do not exceed 5 doses in 24 hours. Ensure accurate weighing using mechanical scales in remote clinics.

title: Snakebite Antivenom Protocols
content: For venomous snakebites (e.g., Viperidae), immobilize the bitten limb and do not apply a tourniquet. Administer polyvalent antivenom immediately if signs of systemic envenomation (coagulopathy, hypotension, neurotoxicity) are present.

title: Asthma Exacerbation in Remote Clinic
content: For acute asthma exacerbation, start with short-acting beta-agonists (SABA) like Albuterol via nebulizer or MDI. Add oral corticosteroids (e.g., Prednisone 40-50 mg/day) early. Monitor oxygen saturation continuously.
